title: Zehneria micrantha
taxonomy: Zehneria micrantha belongs to the family Cucurbitaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Zehneria. The genus Zehneria is further classified under the order Cucurbitales. Zehneria micrantha is native to tropical Asia, specifically in India and Southeast Asia. The classification of Zehneria micrantha is consistent with the APG III classification system.
uses: Zehneria micrantha is known to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. It has been traditionally used to treat fever, cough, and cold. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing swelling and pain. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Zehneria micrantha for medicinal purposes.

intro: Zehneria micrantha, also known as the small-fruited cordia, is a plant species belonging to the family Cucurbitaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate oxidative stress, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities, contributing to its use in treating various health conditions.
